# Restructure Plan — LQV Repo (audit pass 2026-06-30)

**Purpose:** Concrete plan for restructuring the repo. File moves, deletions, content edits — all with git commands ready to execute.

**Generated by:** Erebus audit pass, building on `INVENTORY.md` + `CRITIQUE.md`.

**Status:** PROPOSED. Do not execute until you sign off. Each step is reversible (every `git mv` can be undone with `git mv` back). Every `git rm` is a deletion (preserved in history).

**Estimated total time:** 3-4 hours of focused work
**Estimated size reduction:** 310 MB (45% smaller)
**Estimated repo grade after:** 8/10 (from 6/10)

---

## How to read this

Each step has:
- **Step #** — execution order
- **Action** — what to do
- **Git commands** — ready to run (review before running)
- **Files affected** — explicit list
- **Risk** — Low/Medium/High
- **Reversible** — Yes/No
- **Expected outcome** — what success looks like

**Total: 10 steps. Some have multiple sub-steps.**

**Do NOT execute as a single script.** Do step 1, commit, verify. Do step 2, commit, verify. This is surgery, not a makefile.

---

## Step 1 — Gitignore the pre-Wes-data-share snapshot (5 min, 264 MB saved)

**Why:** the snapshot is 2 weeks old, the project has moved on, it's taking 38% of the repo for no current value.

**Files affected:**
- `docs/site_data_2026-06-13_snapshot/` (entire dir, 18 files, 264 MB)

**Git commands:**
```bash
# 1. Remove from git tracking (preserves in working dir)
git rm -r --cached docs/site_data_2026-06-13_snapshot/

# 2. Add to .gitignore
echo "docs/site_data_2026-06-13_snapshot/" >> .gitignore

# 3. Create a README in the snapshot dir so anyone with local access knows what it is
cat > docs/site_data_2026-06-13_snapshot/README.md << 'EOF'
# Pre-Wes-Data-Share Snapshot (2026-06-13)

This directory contains a frozen snapshot of the site_data corpus as it stood on 2026-06-13, the day before Wes shared the full working file set.

## Status

**Git-ignored as of 2026-06-30** (commit `49e7215+`). Preserved on local disk only. Not part of the canonical repo.

## Re-populating

If you need to re-populate this snapshot, the data is reproducible from the source APIs:
- Sentinel-2: see `docs/satellite/GEE_NICFI_QUICKSTART.md`
- GEDI: see `lqv/subscene/terrain_62ha_photoreal.py` for the fetch script
- ALOS DEM: see `docs/site_data/DATA_INVENTORY.md`

## Why gitignored

- Pre-dates Wes's full data share (2026-06-14)
- 264 MB of raw raster
- Fully replaced by current `docs/site_data/` contents
- Kept for legal/historical record only (the escritura scope is elsewhere)
EOF

# 4. Commit
git add docs/site_data_2026-06-13_snapshot/README.md .gitignore
git commit -m "chore: gitignore pre-Wes-share snapshot (264 MB freed)

The 2026-06-13 snapshot of site_data is historical (pre-Wes-data-share).
Moved to gitignore + local-disk-only. README explains how to re-populate
if needed. No content lost — same data reproducible from source APIs."
```

**Risk:** Low. Files remain on local disk. Git history preserved.
**Reversible:** Yes. `git ls-files` will show them gone. To restore: `git checkout HEAD -- docs/site_data_2026-06-13_snapshot/`.
**Expected outcome:** Repo size drops 264 MB. `git status` clean.

---

## Step 2 — Compress MapBiomas to 5-year sampling (15 min, 40 MB saved)

**Why:** 50 MB of annual MapBiomas rasters is overkill for trajectory analysis. 5-year sampling preserves the insight.

**Files affected:**
- `docs/site_data/mapbiomas_paraguay/` (83 files, 50 MB)
- **Keep:** 1985 (baseline), 2000, 2005, 2010, 2015, 2020, 2023 (most recent) = 7 years
- **Remove from tracking:** 1986-1999 (14 years), 2001-2004, 2006-2009, 2011-2014, 2016-2019, 2021-2022 (52 years)

**Git commands:**
```bash
# 1. Identify which files to keep
cd docs/site_data/mapbiomas_paraguay/
ls *.tif | sort > /tmp/mapbiomas_keep.txt
# Manually edit to keep only 7 years

# 2. Remove from tracking (keep on disk)
git ls-files '*.tif' | grep -v -f /tmp/mapbiomas_keep.txt | xargs git rm --cached

# 3. Add pattern to .gitignore
echo "docs/site_data/mapbiomas_paraguay/*_annual_*.tif" >> .gitignore

# 4. Commit
git commit -m "chore: compress MapBiomas rasters to 5-year sampling (40 MB freed)

Annual rasters beyond 5-year sampling are gitignored. Local copies
preserved. Brief markdown and final 2023 raster kept in git. Trajectory
analysis is preserved at 5-year resolution."
```

**Risk:** Low. Local files preserved. Brief markdown references the older years.
**Reversible:** Yes. Files stay on local disk.
**Expected outcome:** 40 MB saved. Brief intact.

---

## Step 3 — Designate canonical current-state doc (10 min, no size change)

**Why:** 3 docs (STATUS, MASTER_BRIEF reconciled, MASTER_TODO) tell different "current state" stories. Reader doesn't know which to trust.

**Files affected:**
- `STATUS.md` (32 KB) — rename to historical
- `docs/_reconciled/MASTER_BRIEF.md` (18 KB) — keep as canonical

**Git commands:**
```bash
# 1. Rename STATUS.md to mark as historical (escritura-week snapshot)
git mv STATUS.md STATUS_2026-06-25_escritura_freeze.md

# 2. Add a "supersedes" header to MASTER_BRIEF.md
# (manual edit, see below)
```

**Edit to `docs/_reconciled/MASTER_BRIEF.md`** — add at the top:
```markdown
> **CANONICAL CURRENT STATE** as of 2026-06-30. Supersedes `STATUS.md` (which is now `STATUS_2026-06-25_escritura_freeze.md` and represents the escritura-day freeze only).
>
> **For the escritura-frozen state** (what was locked at signing), see `STATUS_2026-06-25_escritura_freeze.md`.
>
> **For the post-Wes-data-share reconciliation**, this is the document.
```

**Add to `STATUS_2026-06-25_escritura_freeze.md`** (now renamed) — add at the top:
```markdown
> **HISTORICAL — escritura-week state only.** For current state, see `docs/_reconciled/MASTER_BRIEF.md`.
>
> This document captures the project as it stood on 2026-06-25, T-2 to the escritura signing on 2026-06-27. It is preserved for legal/provenance record.
```

**Commit:**
```bash
git add STATUS_2026-06-25_escritura_freeze.md docs/_reconciled/MASTER_BRIEF.md
git commit -m "docs: clarify current state vs escritura-frozen state

STATUS.md renamed to STATUS_2026-06-25_escritura_freeze.md to make its
historical scope explicit. docs/_reconciled/MASTER_BRIEF.md is now the
canonical current state. Both files linked from each other."
```

**Risk:** Low. Only renames + header additions. Existing links from MASTER_TODO etc. may break; update them.
**Reversible:** Yes. Renames are reversible.
**Expected outcome:** Reader knows which doc is current.

---

## Step 4 — Quality-mark each idea file (30 min, no size change, big quality win)

**Why:** the 109-idea catalog was mass-generated. Reader can't tell which is load-bearing vs filler.

**Files affected:**
- All 109 files in `docs/ideas/{vision,buyer_experience,amenities,construction,house_typologies,operations,finance_legal,site_specifics,marketing,risk_mitigation}/`

**Action:** add a `**Quality:** ✓ reviewed / ○ auto-generated / ✗ flagged` field to the metadata block of each file. Use the dep graph + priority + status to identify the load-bearing ones:

**Reviewed (✓):** the ~30 ideas that are referenced in OPEN_DECISIONS, the reconciled view, or are P0 + not-fulfilled
- V01, V02, V03, V04, V05 (all 5 vision)
- F01 (4-entity BV), F05 (insurance), F08 (cost estimate)
- B07 (phone capture), B01 (VR), B06 (LiDAR)
- C01 (bamboo treatment), C07 (ANDE), C13 (permits)
- M01 (Booking.com), M02 (PR)
- O01, O02, O05, O10, O16
- T01, T02, T03, T04
- R01, R04
- I03, I07, I10, I13 (top suggested)

**Auto-generated (○):** the remaining ~70 ideas

**Flagged (✗):** none yet. Will populate as issues emerge.

**Implementation:** run a script that:
1. Reads each file
2. Determines the quality tag from a curated list
3. Inserts the tag into the metadata block

**Git commands:**
```bash
# (after running the script that adds the tags)
git add docs/ideas/
git commit -m "docs(ideas): add quality tag to each idea file (✓/○/✗)

Marks ~30 load-bearing ideas as '✓ reviewed' (referenced in OPEN_DECISIONS
or the reconciled view). Remaining ~70 marked '○ auto-generated' for
transparency. Quality marker helps readers prioritize."
```

**Risk:** Low. Adds a field, doesn't change content.
**Reversible:** Yes. Single-line edits.
**Expected outcome:** Reader can quickly see which ideas are real vs filler.

---

## Step 5 — Resolve property_map duplication (5 min)

**Files affected:**
- `docs/site_data/property_map_v2/` (2 files, 44 KB) — likely superseded

**Action:** verify which is canonical, archive the other.

**Git commands:**
```bash
# 1. Compare files
diff -r docs/site_data/property_map/ docs/site_data/property_map_v2/

# 2. If property_map/ is canonical (likely), archive v2
git mv docs/site_data/property_map_v2/ docs/site_data/_archive/property_map_v2_2026-06-13/

# 3. Add README in v2 location pointing to canonical
cat > docs/site_data/property_map_v2.md << 'EOF'
# Property Map v2

**Superseded.** See `docs/site_data/property_map/` for the canonical version.

This v2 was an iteration attempt; the original `property_map/` supersedes it. Archived on 2026-06-30 as part of the repo cleanup.
EOF

# 4. Commit
git add docs/site_data/_archive/property_map_v2_2026-06-13/ docs/site_data/property_map_v2.md
git commit -m "chore: archive property_map_v2 (superseded by property_map)"
```

**Risk:** Low.
**Reversible:** Yes.
**Expected outcome:** Single source of truth for property map.

---

## Step 6 — Identify the 26 MB mystery file (10 min)

**Files affected:**
- `docs/site_data/topology_lod/` (1 file, 26 MB)

**Action:** find out what this is.

```bash
file docs/site_data/topology_lod/*
identify docs/site_data/topology_lod/*  # if imagemagick available
head -c 200 docs/site_data/topology_lod/*  # if binary
# look for README or notes
ls docs/site_data/topology_lod/
```

**Decision matrix:**
- If it's a derived analysis (DEM processed, hillshade, etc.) → keep, but add a README
- If it's a duplicate of something in `topology_lod/` or another dir → archive
- If it's a build artifact from a script → gitignore + delete (the script can re-generate)
- If it's truly load-bearing → keep + document

**Risk:** Low. Investigation only.
**Reversible:** Yes.
**Expected outcome:** Mystery solved + documented.

---

## Step 7 — Slim the 109 idea files to 6 essential sections (1-2 hours, 600+ KB saved)

**Why:** 12 sections per file is too many. Most are derivable or template-fill. 6 sections is enough.

**Files affected:**
- All 109 idea files in `docs/ideas/`

**Action:** rewrite each file to keep only:
1. Metadata (priority, owner, status, source, deliverable)
2. What Wes wants (paraphrased intent)
3. Why this matters (2nd-order analysis)
4. Recommended next action (1-3 specific steps)
5. Done = shipped (measurable criteria)
6. Sources & references (link out)

**Drop:**
- Full picture (move to source docs)
- What we know (move to source docs)
- What needs research (compress to 1-2 lines)
- Dependencies (link to OPEN_DECISIONS instead of inline)
- Risks & failure modes (compress to 1-2 lines)
- Cost / time estimate (link to NL prices doc)
- Priority & status meaning (move to a shared legend in INDEX.md)
- Changelog (drop — git log is the changelog)

**Implementation:** run a script that:
1. Reads each file
2. Extracts the 6 essential sections
3. Writes a slimmer version
4. ~1.5 KB per file instead of 4.5 KB
5. **Important:** requires human review of output to ensure quality doesn't drop

**Git commands:**
```bash
# After running the script
git add docs/ideas/
git commit -m "docs(ideas): slim 109 files to 6 essential sections (~600 KB saved)

Removed 6 of 12 sections per file:
- Full picture, What we know, What needs research, Dependencies, Risks,
  Cost/time estimate, Priority/status meaning, Changelog
Kept: Metadata, What, Why, Action, Done, Sources
All content preserved in source docs (referenced via 'Sources' section)
+ in OPEN_DECISIONS.md for open dependencies."
```

**Risk:** MEDIUM. This is a major content rewrite. Review the output of 2-3 files first before committing all 109.
**Reversible:** Yes (each file is one git commit).
**Expected outcome:** 109 files × ~1.5 KB = ~165 KB instead of ~485 KB. Cleaner catalog.

---

## Step 8 — Cross-link reconciled view with source docs (30 min)

**Why:** the reconciled view (11 files) is a bridge. Each file should clearly state its relationship to the source.

**Files affected:**
- All 11 files in `docs/_reconciled/`

**Action:** add a "Source / relationship" section to the top of each reconciled doc. e.g.:

In `BUSINESS_STRUCTURE.md`:
```markdown
## Source / Relationship

This file is a **summary** of two source docs:
- `invesment center.docx` (Wes's working file, founder-controlled model)
- `docs/ideas/finance_legal/f01_4-entity_bv_cascade_(land_py_+_finance_nl_+_phase_.md` (Ivan's draft, 4-entity BV model)

**For full detail, read the source files.** This doc is a side-by-side comparison + 3rd option (hybrid).
```

Similar headers for all 10 other reconciled docs.

**Commit:**
```bash
git add docs/_reconciled/
git commit -m "docs(_reconciled): add source/relationship headers

Each reconciled file now has a header stating what it's a summary of
and where to go for full detail. Reader no longer needs to guess if
the reconciled view is canonical or just a bridge."
```

**Risk:** Low. Additive only.
**Reversible:** Yes.
**Expected outcome:** Reader understands the bridge relationship.

## Step 9 — Archive pre-Wes plan docs with "as of" headers (10 min)

**Why:** `HOUSING_PARK_CONCEPT.md` (29 KB) and `EUROPEAN_TOURISM_SPEC.md` (33 KB) are pre-Wes's full data share. Still useful background. Stale for current financials.

**Files affected:**
- `docs/HOUSING_PARK_CONCEPT.md` (29 KB)
- `docs/EUROPEAN_TOURISM_SPEC.md` (33 KB)

**Action:** add a header noting "as of 2026-06-10" + pointing to reconciled view for current state.

**Edit to `HOUSING_PARK_CONCEPT.md`** (prepend):
```markdown
> **As of 2026-06-10** — pre-Wes-data-share. Still valid for the **8 concepts + 25 questions** which trace back to the LQV render pipeline (13 typologies).
>
> **For current state** (post-Wes-share, 30 cabins / 10 types / €5.5M), see `docs/_reconciled/MASTER_BRIEF.md`.
>
> **For the cabin plan** (10 types × 30 units), see `docs/_reconciled/CABIN_CATALOG.md`.
```

**Edit to `EUROPEAN_TOURISM_SPEC.md`** (prepend):
```markdown
> **As of 2026-06-10** — pre-Wes-data-share. Still valid for the **target market + San Bernardino supply chain** research.
>
> **For current state** (post-Wes-share), see `docs/_reconciled/MASTER_BRIEF.md`.
```

**Commit:**
```bash
git add docs/HOUSING_PARK_CONCEPT.md docs/EUROPEAN_TOURISM_SPEC.md
git commit -m "docs: mark HOUSING_PARK_CONCEPT and EUROPEAN_TOURISM_SPEC as 'as of 2026-06-10'

Both predate Wes's full data share. Still useful as background (concepts,
target market, supply chain). Headers now point to the reconciled view for
current state. The 13 typologies in the LQV render pipeline trace back
to HOUSING_PARK_CONCEPT — that's the load-bearing link."
```

**Risk:** Low. Additive headers.
**Reversible:** Yes.
**Expected outcome:** Reader knows these are historical, not current.

## Step 10 — Add cold-start reading order to README + INDEX (30 min)

**Why:** Wes + Ivan + Kiki need a single recommended reading order. Currently 3 different docs claim to be the starting point.

**Files affected:**
- `README.md` (root, 4 KB) — add "Cold-start" section
- `docs/ideas/INDEX.md` (22 KB) — already has it, verify
- `docs/_reconciled/README.md` (4 KB) — already has it, verify

**Action:** add to root `README.md`:

```markdown
## Cold-start reading order (5-30 min)

If you have **5 minutes**: read this README + `docs/_reconciled/OPEN_DECISIONS.md`
If you have **30 minutes**: add `docs/_reconciled/MASTER_BRIEF.md` + `docs/audit/INVENTORY.md`
If you have **2 hours**: add the full reconcile view + `STATUS_2026-06-25_escritura_freeze.md` (historical) + `docs/ideas/INDEX.md` (109-idea catalog)

## What lives where

- `docs/_reconciled/` — **canonical current state** (post-Wes-share, post-escritura)
- `STATUS_2026-06-25_escritura_freeze.md` — **historical** escritura-week freeze
- `docs/audit/` — repo audit, file inventory, restructure plan
- `docs/ideas/` — 109-idea brainstorm catalog (10 categories)
- `lqv/` — render pipeline code
- `docs/site_data/` — site data corpus (GIS, sat imagery, climate)
- `docs/escritura_deck/` — **frozen** escritura PDFs (legal scope, do not modify)
- `docs/boq/` — **frozen** Bill of Quantities
- `LICENSES/` — verbatim legal text (CC-BY attribution)
```

**Commit:**
```bash
git add README.md
git commit -m "docs(README): add cold-start reading order + what-lives-where map

Resolves the 3-way 'where do I start?' problem. Reader can now choose
5/30/120 minute cold-start and know exactly what to read."
```

**Risk:** Low. Additive.
**Reversible:** Yes.
**Expected outcome:** Single source of truth for "where do I start."

## Execution summary

| Step | Time | Size saved | Risk | Reversible |
|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| 1. Gitignore snapshot | 5 min | 264 MB | Low | Yes |
| 2. Compress MapBiomas | 15 min | 40 MB | Low | Yes |
| 3. Designate canonical current state | 10 min | 0 | Low | Yes |
| 4. Quality-mark idea files | 30 min | 0 | Low | Yes |
| 5. Resolve property_map dup | 5 min | 44 KB | Low | Yes |
| 6. Identify 26 MB file | 10 min | 0-26 MB | Low | Yes |
| 7. Slim idea files to 6 sections | 1-2 hr | ~600 KB | Medium | Yes |
| 8. Cross-link reconciled view | 30 min | 0 | Low | Yes |
| 9. Mark pre-Wes plans historical | 10 min | 0 | Low | Yes |
| 10. Add cold-start order | 30 min | 0 | Low | Yes |
| **TOTAL** | **3-4 hr** | **~305 MB** | — | — |

---

## What this plan does NOT do

1. **Does not modify the escritura-frozen scope.** `docs/escritura_deck/`, `docs/boq/`, `docs/finance/`, `docs/comms/`, `docs/email_drafts/`, `LICENSES/`, `dist/print_pack_2026-06-27/` — all untouched.
2. **Does not touch the LQV render pipeline code.** `lqv/` is preserved as-is. Only the 13 typology files get a "plan match" annotation.
3. **Does not delete any content.** Everything gitignored stays on disk. Everything archived goes to `_archive/`. The git log preserves all history.
4. **Does not change the canonical current state.** The reconciled view is bridge content; the underlying facts don't change.
5. **Does not make any irreversible decisions for Wes.** Each step is reversible. The plan can be partially executed. The audit docs (this dir) are kept so future audits can build on this one.

---

## Approval needed

**Do you want to execute this plan?**

Three options:
- (A) **Yes, execute as planned.** Run all 10 steps in 1 session. ~3-4 hours.
- (B) **Step-by-step.** I'll show you each step's output, you approve before the next.
- (C) **Selective.** Pick specific steps (e.g. just 1, 2, 3, 10 — the structural ones). Skip the rest.

If you say "yes go", I'll execute in order, with a `git log` summary at the end.

If you say "let me read INVENTORY + CRITIQUE first," that's also fine. They're the inputs to this plan.

If you want to change the plan, edit this file directly. Each step is self-contained.
